Title: The role of GDP-L-galactose phosphorylase in the control of ascorbate biosynthesis

Common_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Endogenous promoter including 5' UTR in complementation construct
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Antioxidant and redox metabolite important for plant growth and stress responses
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: High vegetative expression mentioned for GGP genes
Common_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Cytosol and nucleus via GGP protein data
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: T-DNA mutant complementation (vtc2-4) with GGP-GFP; ascorbate measurement
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Loss of VTC2 lowers ascorbate; complementation restores WT ascorbate
Common_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: VTC5 as paralog in model context
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Exogenous ascorbate is relevant to GGP double-mutant rescue in cited background
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. VTC2 is a major GGP isoform contributing to ascorbate biosynthesis. 2. vtc2 mutant has reduced ascorbate but is viable because of paralogous VTC5. 3. Complementation with GGP-GFP restores WT ascorbate.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Important allele/gene target for increasing vitamin C, especially by overcoming translational repression
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: In this paper VTC2 is effectively treated as the principal GGP gene tested experimentally

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Antioxidant and redox metabolite important for plant growth and stress responses
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Modeling and mutant context discussed from previous studies
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Provides paralogous GGP activity but does not fully compensate VTC2 loss; double mutants arrest growth
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Exogenous ascorbate can complement vtc2 vtc5 double mutant growth arrest as cited
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. VTC5 is a paralog of VTC2/GGP. 2. The model incorporated VTC2 and VTC5 as parallel GGP reactions. 3. The paper notes that the two isoforms do not compensate each other when knocked out.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential auxiliary target together with VTC2/GGP in engineering strategies
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Not directly assayed experimentally here as an independent construct; included because the paper explicitly analyzes its contribution in pathway control context

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Endogenous GGP promoter including 5' UTR; CaMV35S constitutive promoter
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Core L-galactose branch of ascorbate biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Antioxidant and redox metabolite important for plant growth and stress responses
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Forms a direct precursor of the terminal product and controls flux toward ascorbate
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Established as the main control point with high flux and concentration control
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Mutant complementation in Arabidopsis vtc2; transient overexpression in Nicotiana benthamiana; metabolite profiling; immunoblot; kinetic modeling/metabolic control analysis
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Among the highest expressed SW genes in vegetative tissue at mRNA level, but protein abundance is very low
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Transiently well expressed in N. benthamiana from 35S; in Arabidopsis GGP-GFP fluorescence/protein was barely detectable under native promoter
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Standard growth conditions; transient agroinfiltration; exogenous ascorbate considered in modeling feedback repression
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Overexpression increased leaf ascorbate about 2- to 3-fold; a very small amount of protein restored WT ascorbate in vtc2 mutant
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GGP was the only SW enzyme whose individual overexpression consistently increased ascorbate. 2. Native-promoter GGP-GFP protein accumulated at extremely low abundance yet complemented vtc2, indicating strong control despite low protein level. 3. Kinetic modeling showed GGP has the dominant flux and concentration control due to low abundance plus ascorbate feedback repression.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Transcriptome database mining from TRAVA and eFP-seq Browser for mRNA expression; metabolite profiling
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: High-value target for biofortification of vitamin C; increasing GGP expression or relieving 5'UTR/uORF repression is predicted to be more effective than manipulating other pathway enzymes
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Arabidopsis has two GGP isoforms, VTC2 and VTC5; discussion notes conserved uORF-mediated translational repression and prior CRISPR editing of the uORF in multiple species increases ascorbate, though those editing data are cited from previous studies, not generated here

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Endogenous GME promoter; CaMV35S constitutive promoter
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Upstream nucleotide-sugar interconversion in the L-galactose pathway
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Antioxidant and redox metabolite important for plant growth and stress responses
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Supplies the direct substrate pool for GGP and thus supports ascorbate biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Not established as major control point in this study
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Mutant complementation in Arabidopsis gme; transient overexpression in Nicotiana benthamiana; metabolite profiling; immunoblot; localization; interaction assays
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: High transcript expression in vegetative tissue according to public databases
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Strong GFP signal in Arabidopsis seedlings and transient expression in N. benthamiana
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Exogenous ascorbate did not rescue gme lethality
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: GME-GFP partially complemented ascorbate deficiency but not to WT levels; overexpression alone did not increase ascorbate
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GME is essential and loss-of-function is lethal, indicating roles beyond ascorbate synthesis. 2. Functional GME-GFP complemented sterility defects but only partially restored ascorbate. 3. Increased GME alone did not raise ascorbate, though coexpression with GGP sometimes enhanced the GGP effect.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Transcriptome database mining from TRAVA and eFP-seq Browser; metabolite profiling
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Limited standalone engineering value for increasing ascorbate; may be considered only in combination with GGP and with caution because of additional essential roles
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: gme lethality was not rescued by exogenous ascorbate, supporting non-ascorbate functions such as cell wall-related processes

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Endogenous GPP promoter; CaMV35S constitutive promoter
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Antioxidant and redox metabolite important for plant growth and stress responses
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Forms an immediate downstream precursor of ascorbate
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Not established; little control in this study/model
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Mutant complementation in Arabidopsis vtc4; transient overexpression in Nicotiana benthamiana; metabolite profiling; immunoblot; localization; interaction assays
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Strong GFP accumulation in Arabidopsis and N. benthamiana
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Functional GPP-GFP restored WT ascorbate in vtc4 mutant, but overexpression did not increase ascorbate in N. benthamiana
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GPP is required for normal ascorbate biosynthesis because GPP-GFP complements the vtc4 defect. 2. Elevated GPP abundance alone or in combinations without GGP did not increase ascorbate. 3. The enzyme localizes to cytosol and nucleus and associates with neighboring SW enzymes in vivo.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Low priority single-gene target for vitamin C enhancement compared with GGP
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The viability of vtc4 may rely on other enzymes with GPP activity, but this paper still validates GPP function by complementation

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Endogenous L-GalDH promoter; CaMV35S constitutive promoter
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Late cytosolic steps of L-galactose pathway
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Antioxidant and redox metabolite important for plant growth and stress responses
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Forms the immediate precursor of the final mitochondrial oxidation step to ascorbate
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Not established; low control in this study/model
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Rescue of lgaldh mutant with exogenous ascorbate; mutant complementation with L-GalDH-GFP; transient overexpression in Nicotiana benthamiana; localization; interaction assays
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Lower protein abundance than several other SW proteins but functional; fluorescence in cytoplasm and nuclei-like structures
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Exogenous ascorbate rescues lgaldh mutant growth arrest
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Essential for ascorbate biosynthesis, but increasing its expression did not raise ascorbate concentration
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. L-GalDH activity is essential because lgaldh mutants are ascorbate auxotrophs rescued by exogenous ascorbate. 2. Native-promoter L-GalDH-GFP fully complemented lgaldh and restored WT ascorbate. 3. Overexpression alone or with enzymes other than GGP did not significantly elevate ascorbate.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Not an effective standalone target for raising ascorbate under tested conditions
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Competitive inhibition by ascorbate was discussed in the model, but its physiological importance may be uncertain according to cited literature
